ops.title: D3b verdict: NO-GO — keep llama.cpp, do not swap to Rapid-MLX/Qwen3.6-4bit
ops.body:
Live fidelity bake-off (2026-07-15, ~$43 of Cerebras reference spend) on stream-trade's own corpus.
Artifact: `backtests/fidelity/go_no_go.rapidmlx.md` (**LOCAL ONLY** — `backtests/` is gitignored).

## Verdict: NO-GO. Keep llama.cpp. Do not swap the serve engine.

Config tested: rapid-mlx 0.10.9 serving `unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-MLX-4bit` on :8400.
NOTE: use the full model id, NOT the `qwen3.6-35b` alias — rapid-mlx 404s the alias and that yields a spurious 0 f1 (a false NO-GO). This run used the correct id.

| gate | cerebras (ref) | local | result |
|---|---|---|---|
| hallucinations (HARD) | 6 | **27** | FAIL |
| f1 | 0.250 | 0.185 | FAIL |
| only-local (invented) | — | 23 (max 1) | FAIL |
| enter.stop field | 1.000 | 0.500 | FAIL |
| tool-schema survival (HARD) | — | 0 dispatch errors | **PASS** |
| recall | 0.182 | 0.227 | PASS |

Tool-schema survival passed cleanly, confirming the earlier corrected rule (the discriminator is whether the engine's tool-call extraction matches the served model's NATIVE tool format — not grammar-vs-parser). That was never the binding constraint.

## What actually killed it

27 hallucinations vs a historical local baseline of 0–5 (see `llamacpp-subset6.md`, `go_no_go.md`). Roughly 5x the worst prior local. The model sprays invented events.

## Confound — do not over-read this result

The run changed THREE variables at once vs the historical baseline: engine (llama.cpp → rapid-mlx), quant (Q8 → 4bit), and model (gpt-oss → Qwen3.6-35B-A3B). The planned llama-server Q8 comparison arm **never ran** (the agent died after writing the rapid-mlx artifact), so quant-vs-model is undiagnosed. This does not change the action — the exact proposed config is NO-GO — but a future retry should isolate one variable.

## Harness caveat worth its own look

Cerebras — the REFERENCE — scores f1 0.250, matching only 4 of 22 events, with 6 hallucinations of its own. Across every artifact on disk the reference lands f1 0.154–0.250. A reference that weak means the gate may be measuring ground-truth misalignment or a broken event matcher rather than model quality. The NO-GO is still safe (it preserves the status quo), but treat these absolute numbers as suspect.

## Scope note

This does NOT block D3c U-SHIP. The serve-engine swap and the athome cache cutover are independent: D3c ships `athome.serve` wiring pointed at llama-server instead of rapid-mlx.
